title: Quiz: Molecular Docking
description: Test your understanding of molecular docking principles and methods

questions.question: What is the primary goal of molecular docking?

options.text: Predict the preferred binding pose and estimate affinity

questions.explanation: Molecular docking predicts how a small molecule ligand binds to a protein target (the pose) and estimates the relative binding affinity. It does not calculate exact binding energies or determine structures.

questions.question: Which search algorithm builds the ligand incrementally in the binding site?
questions.explanation: Incremental construction, used by FlexX and DOCK, fragments the ligand, places an anchor fragment, then builds the rest of the molecule step by step. GA, MC, and SA work with complete ligand conformations.

questions.question: What type of scoring function is derived from statistical analysis of the PDB?
questions.explanation: Knowledge-based scoring functions (PMF, DrugScore) derive potentials from statistical analysis of atom-atom distances in the PDB. They assume that frequently observed distances are energetically favorable.

questions.question: What RMSD threshold is typically considered successful for redocking validation?
questions.explanation: An RMSD < 2.0 Å between the docked pose and crystallographic pose is generally considered successful redocking. This indicates the docking method can reproduce the experimental binding mode.

questions.question: Docking scores correlate well with experimental binding affinities (Kd values).
questions.explanation: False. Docking scores are useful for ranking compounds within a series but generally show poor correlation with actual Kd values. They are designed for pose prediction and relative ranking, not absolute affinity prediction.

questions.question: Which are challenges for standard docking methods? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Protein flexibility, covalent binding, and metal coordination are all challenging for standard docking. Small rigid ligands are actually easier to dock due to limited conformational search required.
